title: normalize/case pinned to shipped Unicode
manager: Case-folding and normalization (NFC/NFD and friends) pinned to a specific shipped Unicode version, so "uppercase this" and "are these two strings equal after normalization" give the same answer on every host and never drift when a runtime updates its Unicode tables underneath you. Unpinned Unicode is a silent cross-host correctness bug — the same machine disagreeing with itself on two computers — and this nails it shut.
cs: normalize + case ops pinned to a bundled Unicode version, not the host's; cross-host determinism now, full cross-host conformance certification later (v11); interacts with the `unicode: N` attribute (W6.59).
